description: A comprehensive museum showcasing Bashu culture and Chengdu's history, offering insights into the profound heritage of the Land of Abundance.
A folk-custom commercial street where visitors can experience Western Sichuan folk traditions and traditional snack culture.
The largest comprehensive museum in Sichuan Province, featuring extensive displays of Bashu culture, ethnic relics, and fine arts.
A well-known snack hub near the University of Electronic Science and Technology, gathering various Chengdu specialty snacks and night market culture.
A UNESCO World Heritage Site and an outstanding example of ancient hydraulic engineering.
The only temple in China dedicated to both ruler and subjects, an important site showcasing Three Kingdoms culture.
A century-old teahouse in People's Park, a classic venue to experience authentic Chengdu tea culture.
The premier Taoist temple in Western Sichuan, originally built during the Tang Dynasty, known for its rich Taoist culture, exquisite architecture, and thriving incense offerings.
A preserved Qing Dynasty street district, a classic alleyway to experience the slow-paced lifestyle of old Chengdu.
One of the birthplaces of Taoism in China, a famous Taoist mountain known for its tranquility.
A symbolic ancient bridge in Dujiangyan, offering beautiful nighttime views when illuminated, and an important attraction in Dujiangyan Ancient City.
A landmark building in Chengdu, featuring a rooftop panda sculpture that has become a popular photo spot.
A famous food street in Chengdu, gathering a variety of Sichuan cuisine snacks, making it an excellent place to experience Chengdu's vibrant street food culture.
A leisure center for Chengdu residents, featuring Heming Teahouse for a slow-paced lifestyle experience and traditional services like ear-cleaning.
Chengdu's most bustling commercial district, a core shopping and entertainment hub.
